title: useThrottle: rate-limit value updates
scenario_context: Scroll position updates 60 times/sec. Network requests off scroll need throttling to 4Hz.
verification_chain: Layer 7: throttled value updates at most every N ms
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No trailing edge — last value lost
Throttling the callback not the value
what_breaks_naive_approach: Without throttle: 60 setStates/sec, parent re-renders cascade. With throttle: smooth + responsive.
reasoning: Track lastUpdate timestamp. If now-last >= delay, update immediately. Else schedule trailing update.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Throttle != debounce. 2) Trailing edge for final value. 3) Cleanup.

title: useFocusTrap for modals and dialogs
scenario_context: Modal opens; Tab should cycle within modal, not into background.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Screen reader users navigate into background page.
reasoning: On mount, query focusables. Tab/Shift+Tab cycles. Restore previous active on unmount.
edge_cases_addressed: Modal with no focusables — focus container
test_strategy: Tab cycles in modal; restored on close
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Restore focus. 2) Re-query for dynamic. 3) Initial focus set.

title: useTimeout with cancel and reset
scenario_context: Show toast for 3s. User clicks 'Stay' — cancel timer.
reasoning: Ref for callback. set/clear/reset returned. Cleanup on unmount.
test_strategy: Fake timers; cancel before fire; reset extends
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Ref for fresh callback. 2) Cancel + reset API. 3) Cleanup.

title: Health check endpoints: liveness vs readiness
scenario_context: Kubernetes probes need /live (process up) and /ready (can serve traffic). Different semantics.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Probes that wake on every request
what_breaks_naive_approach: DB down → readiness fails → K8s restarts pod (doesn't help, DB still down).
reasoning: Live = is JS event loop alive. Ready = can serve (DB connected, migrations applied). Restart-policy differs.
frontend_solution: // N/A
test_strategy: Kill DB; readiness fails; liveness ok
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Live vs ready distinction. 2) K8s lifecycle alignment. 3) No auth on probes.

title: Error boundary that reports + offers recovery via app router
scenario_context: Error in route component; show fallback with 'Go to dashboard' that uses router not full reload.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Full reload to recover — loses state
what_breaks_naive_approach: User stuck on error page; reload loses unrelated state.
reasoning: resetKey={pathname}; navigation re-renders with new key, boundary clears. Recovery actions navigate within router.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-004 RouteBoundary with useLocation
edge_cases_addressed: Error in nested route — local boundary
Error in error fallback — fail to root
test_strategy: Throw in route; click recovery; navigates
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Router-integrated recovery. 2) Reset key. 3) Layered boundaries.

title: Discriminated union for paginated list responses
scenario_context: List API may return empty/has-next/last-page distinctly. Discriminated union forces handling.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User loads page; empty array — was it 'no data' or 'last page'?
reasoning: Variants: {kind: 'empty'}, {kind: 'page', items, nextCursor}, {kind: 'last', items}. Exhaustive switch.
frontend_solution: // useInfiniteList branches on kind
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Discriminated. 2) Empty distinct. 3) Exhaustive.

title: Per-tenant cron schedules
scenario_context: Tenants want daily report at their own time. Need per-tenant cron stored.
verification_chain: Layer 3: worker iterates tenants at minute granularity
what_breaks_naive_approach: Reports at 3am UTC — wrong time for half customers.
reasoning: Per-tenant TZ + cron expression. Master cron fires every minute, evaluates which tenants have matching expr.
frontend_solution: // schedule picker with timezone
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Per-tenant TZ. 2) Cron expr stored. 3) Minute resolution.

title: Free trial conversion with reminder emails
scenario_context: 14-day trial. Need: countdown UI, reminder emails (day 7, 12, 13), auto-convert or downgrade.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User trial ends; account locked; churn.
reasoning: Trial → reminders → if payment method on file: convert; else: downgrade to free.
frontend_solution: // TrialBanner with countdown + CTA
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Multi-touch reminders. 2) Auto-convert or downgrade. 3) Funnel tracked.

title: Welcome email with personalized next steps
scenario_context: Signup email is template. Personalize based on signup answer (company size, role).
reasoning: Email body branches on user profile.
frontend_solution: // signup form captures role + company size
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Personalized. 2) Trackable. 3) Multipart.

title: Per-resource sharing with link tokens
scenario_context: User shares specific project via link with view-only rights. Link token grants scoped access.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Token in URL hash — analytics leak
reasoning: Token bound to (resource, permission, expiry). On access, hydrate scoped session.
frontend_solution: // ShareDialog with link + permission + expiry
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Scoped tokens. 2) Expiry default. 3) Revocable.

title: Synchronized brush across multiple charts
scenario_context: Two charts share x-axis. Brush on one filters both.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User brushes one, other stays full.
reasoning: Shared filter context; both charts read.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-058 with shared context
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Shared state. 2) Debounced. 3) Reset across.

title: Visx zoom + pan with bounded extent
scenario_context: Custom chart with zoom — limit extent so user doesn't lose data.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Mouse wheel without modifier — scroll conflict
reasoning: @visx/zoom with scale bounds + reset button + double-click to reset.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-018 with Zoom bounds
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Bounded. 2) Reset visible. 3) Keyboard alternative.

title: Chart.js polar area chart with category coloring
scenario_context: Compare metrics across categories. Polar area > pie for non-proportional totals.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pie shows proportion of total — polar area shows magnitude per category.
reasoning: chart type polarArea, distinct colors, optional radial axis ticks for scale.
frontend_solution: // new Chart(ctx, { type: 'polarArea', data: {...}, options: { scales: { r: { beginAtZero: true } } } });
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Right chart for data. 2) Radial scale. 3) Distinct colors.

title: ECharts data zoom with throttled updates
scenario_context: DataZoom slider updates on every pixel of drag → expensive series re-render.
reasoning: dataZoom config throttle: 200ms. Final 'datazoomEnd' event fires once.
frontend_solution: // option.dataZoom = [{ throttle: 200, type: 'slider' }];
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Throttle. 2) End event for commit. 3) Performance budget.

title: Tremor table with sorting and pagination
scenario_context: Tremor Table component lacks built-in sort. Need wrapper with controlled state.
reasoning: Server-side sort via API. Header dispatches. Pagination follows.
frontend_solution: // SortableTable wraps Tremor Table with sortable headers
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server-side sort. 2) Coordinated with pagination. 3) URL.

title: Localized number/date formatting in charts
scenario_context: International users; charts show numbers/dates. Hardcoded en-US misleads.
reasoning: Intl.NumberFormat(locale).format(value). Intl.DateTimeFormat for dates. User locale from settings.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Intl API. 2) User locale source. 3) Currency aware.

title: Adding rich text editor: schema validation + sanitization end-to-end
scenario_context: Notes feature uses TipTap. Need: HTML sanitization, length limit, image upload, persistence.
verification_chain: Layer 0: shared schema for safe HTML
what_breaks_naive_approach: User pastes script tag → stored → renders on others' screens → XSS.
reasoning: Allow whitelist of tags/attrs server-side via DOMPurify. Length cap. Images uploaded to storage, referenced.
frontend_solution: // TipTap with image upload + on-paste sanitize
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server-side sanitize. 2) Whitelist not blacklist. 3) Images out-of-band.

title: What if migration fails partway — atomic + rollback
scenario_context: DB migration adds column + backfill. Halfway through, container OOMs. Need: idempotent migration that resumes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single transaction for huge backfill — locks
reasoning: Migration in chunks. State table records progress. Re-run continues from last checkpoint.
frontend_solution: // N/A
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Idempotent + chunked. 2) State persisted. 3) Resumable.

title: Schema-driven mock server for tests
scenario_context: Tests run without real server. Mock generated from Zod schemas — types stay in sync.
verification_chain: Test infra: MSW handlers from schemas
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No validation that mock matches schema
what_breaks_naive_approach: Schema changes → tests pass with stale mocks → prod breaks.
reasoning: Use zod-mock or @faker-js to generate from schema. MSW handlers serve. Tests validate mock against schema first.
test_strategy: Schema change breaks mocks if drifted
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Auto-generated. 2) Schema validates mock. 3) Stable seed.

title: Tenant-scoped search index (Meilisearch/Algolia)
scenario_context: Search across projects. External index must respect tenant isolation.
verification_chain: Layer 1: index per tenant or document with tenantId filter
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sync only on create, not on delete
reasoning: Single index with tenantId as required filter. Sync via DB hooks. Atomic delete on remove.
frontend_solution: // search input with results
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Mandatory filter. 2) Sync hooks. 3) Test isolation.

title: Refunds with audit and tax adjustment
scenario_context: Customer requests refund. Full or partial. Tax adjustments. Audit trail.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Refund without tax adjustment — books wrong.
reasoning: Stripe refund API. Reason required. Audit log. Tax credit auto-handled by Stripe Tax.
frontend_solution: // admin RefundDialog with reason
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Reason required + audit. 2) Tax handled. 3) Idempotent via Stripe.

title: Onboarding A/B test framework
scenario_context: Try 2 onboarding flows. Measure completion. Need assignment + stats.
reasoning: Hash(userId + experiment) → variant. Sticky. Events tagged.
frontend_solution: // useExperiment hook → variant
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Sticky assignment. 2) Tagged events. 3) Stop rules.

title: IP allowlist per tenant for compliance
scenario_context: Enterprise customer wants only their office IPs to access. Per-tenant CIDR list.
reasoning: Validate CIDR entries. Middleware checks real IP (trusted proxy). Empty = allow all (default). Emergency bypass admin endpoint.
frontend_solution: // admin AllowlistEditor with CIDR validation
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) CIDR validation. 2) IPv4+IPv6. 3) Emergency override.

title: Recharts treemap-like via custom rendering
scenario_context: Recharts has Treemap; configure for ratio data.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User can't tell which is which.
reasoning: Treemap with content prop for custom rendering of cells (color, label).
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Custom content. 2) Label only if fits. 3) Tooltip default.

title: Visx geo: world map with custom projections
scenario_context: Visualize global metrics. Mercator distorts areas — alternative projections matter for accuracy.
reasoning: d3-geo projections: equalEarth, naturalEarth1, etc. Visx Mercator/Albers wrappers. Load topo on demand.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Right projection. 2) Topology lazy. 3) Table alt for SR.

title: Chart.js scatter with regression line overlay
scenario_context: Scatter + linear regression. Show trend.
reasoning: simple-statistics lib. Linear regression. r² computed.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Library not manual. 2) r² shown. 3) Edge guard.

title: ECharts pictorial bar for engaging visuals
scenario_context: Marketing dashboard — bars with icon symbols (people, stars). Storytelling.
reasoning: pictorialBar type with symbol path. Repeat to fill.
frontend_solution: // series: [{ type: 'pictorialBar', symbol: 'path://M ...', symbolRepeat: true, data: [...] }]
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Sparingly used. 2) Repeat symbol meaningful. 3) Alt text.

title: Tremor BarList for ranked data
scenario_context: Top 10 leaderboard. Tremor BarList is the right primitive.
reasoning: BarList component: name + value + optional href.
frontend_solution: // <BarList data={topItems.map(t => ({ name: t.label, value: t.count, href: `/items/${t.id}` }))} />
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Use the primitive. 2) href for navigation. 3) Truncate names.

title: Touch interactions on mobile charts
scenario_context: Hover doesn't exist on touch. Tap shows tooltip; tap elsewhere dismisses.
reasoning: Tap toggles. Tap away dismisses. Larger tap targets (>=44px).
frontend_solution: // onClick handler for chart point — set sticky tooltip; click outside resets
edge_cases_addressed: Pinch zoom — preventDefault on chart only
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tap-toggle. 2) Bigger tap targets. 3) Pinch/scroll separation.

title: Adding bulk operations: select all + bulk action
scenario_context: Table needs 'select all matching filter' (could be 50k rows) + delete/move bulk action.
verification_chain: Layer 4: bulk endpoint accepts filter or IDs
reasoning: Backend accepts filter (server resolves). Chunked processing. Job ID for progress.
frontend_solution:
// SelectAllBanner: 'X items on this page selected. Select all Y matching filter?'
// Progress modal for bulk job
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Filter not IDs. 2) Chunked async. 3) Progress UX.
